semester_stress_model.description: Evidence-based semester stress arc for college students
semester_stress_model.source: American College Health Association + Journal of American College Health

weeks.label: Midterm Buildup
weeks.clinical_note: Peak pre-midterm anxiety. Social withdrawal begins.

weeks.label: The Wall
weeks.clinical_note: Hardest week. Cold weather. Shortened daylight. Isolation peaks.

weeks.label: False Recovery
weeks.clinical_note: Midterms over but students depleted. Dangerous period.

weeks.label: Finals Approach
weeks.clinical_note: Final projects colliding. Sleep collapse common.

weeks.label: Finals
weeks.clinical_note: Peak stress. Adrenaline and exhaustion collide.

science_references.sleep: American Academy of Sleep Medicine — 1hr sleep loss = 25% cognitive performance drop
science_references.attendance: Credé et al. 2010, Review of Educational Research — attendance strongest GPA predictor
science_references.isolation: Cacioppo & Hawkley 2009, Trends in Cognitive Sciences — isolation raises cortisol 20%
science_references.nutrition: Yano et al. 2015, Cell — 90% of serotonin produced in gut
science_references.masking: Grandey 2000, Journal of Occupational Health Psychology — masking depletes cognitive resources
